message:
test(ftui-extras): improve live.rs test coverage (bd-1kziq)

Added 14 new tests (27→41 total) covering:
- carriage_return_writes_escape
- with_config_custom_values
- auto_refresh_ignores_nan/negative/infinity_rate
- update_shrinks_height_erases_extra
- empty_update_writes_nothing
- max_height_zero_means_unlimited
- overflow_ellipsis/crop_max_height_1
- live_config_clone/debug
- vertical_overflow_debug/eq

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1l0g9","title":"UX: 80x24 legibility + keyboard accessibility audit","description":"Goal
- Ensure the 80x24 layout remains usable and discoverable with keyboard-only controls.

Deliverables
- Verify key hints are visible at 80x24.
- Ensure collapsed panels still allow sample selection + re-render.
- Add a tiny-size regression snapshot.

Acceptance
- 80x24 is usable without hidden critical controls.
- Snapshot test exists for tiny size with help overlay toggle.

 {"id":"bd-1lzl8","title":"Samples: tagging taxonomy + naming conventions","description":"Goal
- Define a consistent tagging taxonomy and naming conventions for Mermaid samples.

Deliverables
- Tag categories (type, complexity, stress, unicode, labels, nested, dense).
- Naming convention for sample IDs and display names.
- Validation rules enforced by loader or tests.

Acceptance
- Samples follow consistent naming/tagging; filter/search behaves predictably.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:34:48.123812192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:14.347080515Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:14.347062992Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","samples","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","depends_on_id":"bd-2sp01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:34:49.980712071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":794,"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will define the tagging taxonomy and naming conventions for Mermaid samples based on existing sample metadata schema.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:38:04Z"},{"id":796,"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Implementation complete. Added:
1. KNOWN_TAGS constant - 33 tags organized into 6 semantic categories (Structure, Content, Relationships, Density, Rendering, Fallback) with doc comments explaining the taxonomy
2. KNOWN_EDGE_CASE_TAGS constant - 13 edge-case tags for regression coverage tracking
3. 9 new validation tests:
   - all_sample_tags_are_known: validates tags field against KNOWN_TAGS
   - all_sample_edge_cases_are_known: validates edge_cases against KNOWN_EDGE_CASE_TAGS
   - known_tags_are_sorted_within_categories: no duplicates in any tag list
   - sample_ids_are_kebab_case: IDs must be lowercase-dash format
   - sample_ids_start_with_family_prefix: IDs start with family name (skip unsupported)
   - sample_names_are_title_case: names must be Title Case
   - each_sample_has_at_least_one_tag: no empty tags
   - each_sample_has_at_least_one_feature: no empty features
   - known_tags_all_exercised: at most 5 unused tags allowed

All 165 mermaid_showcase tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m0wa","title":"frankenterm-web: stale diff_to_patches fallback","description":"When a BufferDiff is computed for an old geometry and later applied to a resized Buffer, diff coordinates can be out of bounds.  {"id":"bd-21hz","title":"Widget API Frame Integration Tests","description":"## Purpose

Create comprehensive integration tests for the Widget + Frame API to ensure all Frame features work correctly.

## Test Categories

### 1. Frame Feature Tests
- `frame.buffer` access from Widget::render
- `frame.hit_grid` registration and retrieval
- `frame.cursor_position` setting and clearing
- `frame.degradation` level checking

### 2. Widget Category Tests
Each category should have tests:

**Category A (buffer-only):**
- Block renders borders correctly
- Paragraph wraps text
- Rule draws lines

**Category B (hit testing):**
- List registers hit regions for each item
- Table registers hit regions for rows/cells
- Hit IDs are unique and retrievable

**Category C (cursor):**
- TextInput positions cursor correctly
- Cursor visibility toggles work

**Category D (degradation):**
- ProgressBar simplifies at EssentialOnly
- Spinner shows label-only at reduced levels

### 3. Edge Cases
- Empty area (0x0)
- Single cell area (1x1)
- Unicode content with Frame
- Widget nesting with Frame forwarding

## Test Template

```rust
#[test]
fn test_widget_frame_feature() {
    let mut pool = GraphemePool::new();
    let mut frame = Frame::new(80, 24, &mut pool);
    
    // Arrange
    let widget = TestWidget::new();
    let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5);
    
    // Act
    widget.render(area, &mut frame);
    
    // Assert
    assert!(frame.buffer.get(0, 0).symbol == "expected");
    // Add detailed assertions...
}
```

## Success Criteria
- All Frame features have dedicated tests
- Each widget category has test coverage
- Edge cases documented and tested
- Tests include logging for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:36:31.522608704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:44:43.884308586Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:44:43.884289249Z","close_reason":"Added Frame integration tests for Frame + widget features","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Status badge logic checks last_render.fallback_tier. Both adapt to available space. Tests verify render without panic at 120x40, 80x24, and 40x12.","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:38:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-222kz","title":"Perf: re-layout debounce + budget guardrails","description":"Goal
- Prevent runaway re-layout during rapid key input.

Deliverables
- Debounce or coalesce re-layout triggers.
- Budget warnings when layout exceeds threshold; log event with sample/mode.

Acceptance
- Rapid input does not spike CPU; budgets are enforced and logged.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:07:28.223059600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:39:28.118023629Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:39:28.118004062Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","perf","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-222kz","depends_on_id":"bd-3gtf7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:07:30.320977769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":767,"issue_id":"bd-222kz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SilverBridge: Implementation complete. Added debounce (50ms) + budget guardrails (16ms) to MegaRenderCache in mermaid_mega_showcase.rs. Budget-aware debounce: only debounces when previous layout exceeded budget, preserving responsiveness for fast layouts. 130 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:39:24Z"}]}
